refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which is somewhat vague and could imply implementation details. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the functional capability without prescribing how. The FR contains a single obligation focused on energy efficiency target achievement, so it is atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use and throughput
Design energy-efficient mechanical and electrical subsystems to reduce baseline consumption
Enable user-configurable energy efficiency targets with automated adjustment mechanisms
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control logic tightly coupled with energy measurement accuracy
Dependency on operational policies for control parameter limits
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates measurement and control functions, supporting modularity. Coupling risks mainly arise from integration with operational policies, which should be managed at the implementation module level.

assumptions_risks: Energy measurement accuracy is sufficient to reliably assess energy_per_ton
Operational policies remain stable and do not conflict with control adjustments
Customer-defined energy targets are realistic and achievable given system constraints
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for integrating operational policies to ensure alignment between control actions and safety/operational constraints.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with external systems or components for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Contains physical energy consumption but lacks direct control and monitoring logic, causing moderate diffusion.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy monitoring; high diffusion expected.
Safety may use energy data but is not responsible for monitoring or control; high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, as it centralizes energy management functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause interface complexity.
If energy control impacts safety, coordination with Safety Services is required, risking cross-module coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to separate pure monitoring (in NGHE Solution) from control actions that may involve Vehicle or Safety Services, improving modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target by enabling measurement and control to meet the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target criterion.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause interface complexity.
Coordination with Safety Services for control actions impacting safety may cause cross-module coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion, centralizing energy management functions. Other modules have moderate to high diffusion scores, indicating some risk but overall containment is appropriate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data and network link interfaces providing real-time energy consumption and control data, enabling measurement against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The NGHE Solution module is clearly defined as the owner of the DP implementation, with well-specified interfaces to external systems and other modules, supporting clear allocation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on the Vehicle module to reduce interface complexity.
Establish clear coordination protocols with Safety Services to manage cross-module coupling risks.

Consider refining the DP to separate pure monitoring functions (within NGHE Solution) from control actions that may involve Vehicle or Safety Services if containment risks increase, to improve modular clarity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with an appropriate design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and manageable diffusion risks, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although some interface complexity and cross-module coupling risks exist, these are being managed through recommended revisions rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable architecture is preferable to further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: Monitor and manage dependencies on the Vehicle module and coordination with Safety Services to reduce interface complexity and cross-module coupling risks; consider refining DP to separate monitoring and control functions if containment risks increase.

assumptions: The current DP and IM sufficiently satisfy the FR acceptance criteria.
Interface complexity and coupling risks can be managed through coordination protocols and monitoring.
Verification methods via telemetry and network interfaces are reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module may increase interface complexity.
Coordination with Safety Services may cause cross-module coupling impacting safety.
Potential need to refine DP if containment risks escalate.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data confirming real-time energy consumption and control feedback.
Interface performance and reliability metrics between NGHE Solution, Vehicle module, and Safety Services.
Verification results demonstrating compliance with energy_per_ton ≤ E_target criterion.
